$comment: Holo XXX — canonical registration is via the hub catalog (tools/gen-app-defs.mjs). This file mirrors the player's shape so the app is launchable; regenerate from the catalog when wiring into the launcher.
name: Holo XXX
summary: The open web's adult catalogue, discovered by metadata and owned by κ — your collection is an encrypted, biometric-sealed κ-DAG you carry, streamed in 4K/60 as content-verified objects, not a server you run.

description.p: Stash conflates two jobs into one always-on server: organizing a collection and serving it from a machine. Holo XXX splits them. Organizing is attaching metadata edges to content; serving is streaming bytes. Hologram already does both as κ operations — and decouples them from any server.

A scene is one object: an MSE κ-feed (an init segment + ordered media segments, each a κ) plus a metadata edge (title, performers, studio, tags, cover) scraped from the open community catalogues. The whole collection has one root κ, sealed at rest under a TEE-derived key and unlocked only by a biometric ceremony. Move machines, share, fork, time-travel — by one κ.

ul: The open web's metadata DBs are the catalogue — nothing to host
4K/60 (and beyond) streamed as BLAKE3 κ-objects, every byte re-derived before the decoder (L5)
Wide-range categories auto-faceted from scraped tags — instant search and navigation
Encrypted at rest, biometric-gated, no account, no telemetry, selective-disclosure tags
Share one κ-link to a curated playlist — recipient streams it peer-to-peer, byte-verified, neither party hosting
Acquisition is the user's call (yt-dlp / owned files) — the OS organizes, addresses, encrypts and streams; it never hosts or redistributes others' content

releases.description: P0: scene hub + StashDB metadata provider, owned-content provider, sealed κ-scene manifest, manufacture/share/open-on-fresh-device, and the immersive κ-stream catalogue.
